rulings.Rulings: DENY MOTION TO DISMISS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RENEW. (PB)

rulings.Rulings: DEMURRER OVERRULED. MASTER PLEADING SYSTEM PROPERLY UTILIZED. (AA)

rulings.Case Title: GROESSLER VS ACKERMANN
rulings.Rulings: DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON LEGAL MALPRACTICE. TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO CONTINUED LEGAL REPRESENTATION. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON BREACH OF IMPLIED COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH. DENY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION BASED ON FRAUD PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION (PARAGRAPH 11) STATES DAMAGES EXIST. "ACKERMANN HAS IMPROPERLY ENRICHED HIMSELF WITH $3 MILLION OF WHAT WAS, RIGHTFULLY, MY MONEY." (AA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Alternative Mtn To Stay Hearing And Decision Of Defts Alternative Mtn To Quash Until After Jurisdictional Discovery Should Be Complete; Memo
rulings.Rulings: OFF CALENDAR - NO ADDITIONAL BRIEFS FILED, AND JUDGE BORICK EXTENDED TIME FOR SERVICE TO 11-26-02 PER 8-30 ORDER. (JH)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES E MILLER VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. COURT FINDS DECLARATION OF CATALANO INADMISSIBLE BECAUSE CONFLICTING WITH TESTIMONY AT DEPOSITION. SPECIFICALLY CATALANO AT DEPOSITION UNABLE TO IDENTIFY KELLY-MOORE PRODUCTS WHEN PLAINTIFF AND CATALANO PRESENT. (AA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Montrose'S Motion To Seal Or Alternatively Continue Defts' Motions For 1)Summary Judgment, 2)Demurrer, 3)For Security
rulings.Rulings: THE MOTION FOR AN UNDERTAKING IS GRANTED, BOND OF $10,000.00. (JH)

rulings.Rulings: THE MOTION TO SEAL RECORDS IS GRANTED. (JH)

rulings.Case Title: MONTROSE & COMPANY, LLC VS. TOWNSEND WALKER et al
rulings.Rulings: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ABOUND, AND THE CLAIM FOR DAMAGES IS NOT DEFEATED. THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS THE MOTION DOES NOT SEEK TO ADJUDICATE THE FIVE CAUSES OF ACTION INDIVIDUALLY. (JH)

rulings.Case Title: MONTROSE & COMPANY, LLC VS. TOWNSEND WALKER et al
rulings.Rulings: DEMURRER OVERRULED. THE TRADE SECRETS ARE SUFFICIENTLY IDENTIFIED, AND THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT FAIL FOR UNCERTAINTY AS DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT IT POSSESSESS THE AGREEMENTS IN QUESTION. (JH)

rulings.Case Title: ACACIA BANK VS. DIAMOND BRIDGE PARTNERS,LLC
rulings.Rulings: DENY, FAILURE TO MAINTAIN BURDEN RE DAMAGES. PARAGRAPH 21 OF SHIPP'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT PLAINTIFF IS OWED "NO LESS" THAN $4,535,231.02. THIS TESTIMONY INDICATES THAT THE AMOUNT OF DAMAGES IS UNSETTLED. INDEED, IN PLAINTIFF'S REPLY, PLAINTIFF CLAIMS THAT THE BALANCE EXCEEDS $4,912,740.02. SEE PG. 6; LL8. (PB)
